description: the-flow's flight-plan overlay in the `harness flow` CLI descriptor format (plan 024 Phase 3; AC-11). the-flow OWNS + ships this schema and supplies it to the CLI via `harness flow create flight-plan --schema <this file>` — there is NO second copy bundled in the CLI (grill 6/7). It extends the bundled shared-core (`flow-core`) field shape (the node id/type/label/status/next required fields + the node/comment/authority/root optionals) and declares ONLY the flight-plan vocabulary below; the CLI's hand-rolled validator (flow-schema.ts) merges the two. This descriptor REPLACES the former JSON-Schema-draft flight-plan.schema.json, which was reference-only (no runtime validator) and could not drive `create`. Node-type meanings: research; deep-research (excursion, branch_of research/plan); spec + plan (the atomic `1b plan` writes ONE unified plan node — `spec` is retained only for legacy split flows that still carry both); workshop (each its OWN node, branch_of plan); adr; backpressure (the pre-coding seam — an optional post-plan excursion off plan, branch_of plan); phase; fix-loop (branch_of a phase); review; merge; harness-boot (the pre-flight seam before a phase); harness-retro (the post-coding seam at each phase end / post-flight at merge). The backpressure/harness-boot/harness-retro nodes are first-class but ENGINE-OWNED — the flow offers them at the Graph edge (see references/harness-seams.md); their `command` fields are ALWAYS router invocations (`/eng-harness-flow --hook …`), never a child-skill name, and per-phase harness nodes are emitted only when the /eng-harness-flow router is installed AND the repo is provisioned. Mutation + render are owned by the CLI: mutate via `harness flow nav/status/add-node/set-node/insert-node/comment`; render via `harness flow render`. the-flow.md is GENERATED from the-flow.json by the CLI renderer and is never hand-edited. `todo`/`skipped` (Phase 4) are the chore lifecycle statuses — they let a flight plan carry chore nodes (cross-cutting upkeep, and the harness-loop seams ridden as chores) per ws-004; the chore `{kind, importance}` validation is shared-core, so it applies here automatically.
